main:
Same-visit dispensing

A Full In-House Pharmacy. Because Your Pet Cannot Wait Three Days.

When your pet is in pain, fighting an infection, or recovering from surgery, they need medication now, not next week. Our on-site pharmacy is stocked, staffed, and ready every shift we are open.

Call (954) 772-0420 Book a Telehealth Consultation
Same-visit medication dispensing Comprehensive emergency formulary Dogs, cats, birds, and exotics Pharmacist-veterinarian coordination
In-house
Why an in-house pharmacy

In an Emergency, Days Matter.

Outside pharmacies and online pet medication services have their place, but they were not built for emergencies. When a pet needs antibiotics for an infection, pain medication after surgery, or a specific drug to manage a critical condition, waiting 24, 48, or 72 hours for delivery is not an option. The medication has to be ready when your pet leaves our doors.

We built our pharmacy around that reality. The medications most commonly needed in emergency care are stocked, available, and dispensed by our team during the same visit your pet is treated. There is no second errand. There is no anxious waiting. Your pet starts their treatment plan immediately.

Your pet leaves with the medication they need. The same visit.
Formulary

What We Keep on Hand for Emergency Care.

Our pharmacy is stocked with the medications our team uses most often in emergency situations, across the species we treat. Specific medications are selected and prescribed by our veterinarians based on each patient's condition, species, weight, and medical history.

Pain Management Medications

Multi-modal pain control options including opioid analgesics, anti-inflammatory medications, and supportive comfort medications tailored to species and condition.

Antibiotics and Antifungals

Broad-spectrum and targeted antimicrobial medications to treat infections from bite wounds, surgical sites, urinary tract infections, respiratory infections, and more.

Anti-Nausea and GI Support

Medications to manage vomiting, control nausea, and support pets recovering from gastrointestinal upset, surgery, or toxin exposure.

Cardiac and Respiratory Medications

Critical-care medications for patients with heart conditions, respiratory distress, or other circulatory issues that need immediate intervention.

Anti-Seizure Medications

Both injectable and oral seizure medications for patients experiencing acute neurological events or transitioning to ongoing management.

Wound and Skin Care

Topical and oral medications for skin infections, hot spots, ear infections, allergic reactions, and post-surgical wound care.

Toxin and Poisoning Treatments

Medications used to manage toxin ingestion, including activated charcoal, anti-emetics, and specific antidotes when applicable.

Species-Specific Medications

Selected medications appropriate for birds, reptiles, rabbits, and other exotic pets. Many emergency hospitals do not stock these. We do.

How it works

How Prescriptions Are Dispensed During Your Visit.

Step 01

Diagnosis & Plan

Once your pet has been examined and we have a clear diagnosis, your veterinarian determines what medications are needed and explains why each one is being recommended.

Step 02

Prepared On-Site

Our team prepares the prescription from our in-house formulary. No outside pharmacy. No mail-order delays.

Step 03

Clear Instructions

You receive clear at-home instructions on dosing, timing, what to watch for, and when to call us with questions.

Step 04

Leave with Care

You walk out with the medication in hand. Your pet's treatment plan starts immediately, not next week.

FAQ

Frequently Asked Questions.

Can't find what you're looking for? Call (954) 772-0420 or book a telehealth consultation.
